HealthPlan -[OFFERS_WELLNESS_PROGRAM_SAVINGS]-> Wellness Program Cost Reduction Notice
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> Your Grievance and Appeals Rights: There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Value Standards -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Language Assistance Services -[SUMMARY]-> [Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number].] [Tagalog (Tagalog): Kung kailangan ninyo ang tulong sa Tagalog tumawag sa [insert telephone number].] [Chinese (中文): 如果需要中文的帮助，请拨打这个号码 [insert telephone number].] [Navajo (Dine): Dinek'ehgo shika at'ohwol ninisingo, kwijjigo holne' [insert telephone number].] —To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section.—
Coverage Example Scenarios -[ABOUT_COVERAGE_EXAMPLES]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage.

HealthPlan -[OFFERS_WELLNESS_PROGRAM_SAVINGS]-> Wellness Program Cost Reduction Notice
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> Your Grievance and Appeals Rights: There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Value Standards -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Language Assistance Services -[SUMMARY]-> [Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number].] [Tagalog (Tagalog): Kung kailangan ninyo ang tulong sa Tagalog tumawag sa [insert telephone number].] [Chinese (中文): 如果需要中文的帮助，请拨打这个号码 [insert telephone number].] [Navajo (Dine): Dinek'ehgo shika at'ohwol ninisingo, kwijjigo holne' [insert telephone number].] —To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section.—
Coverage Example Scenarios -[ABOUT_COVERAGE_EXAMPLES]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage.

HealthPlan -[OFFERS_WELLNESS_PROGRAM_SAVINGS]-> Wellness Program Cost Reduction Notice
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> Your Grievance and Appeals Rights: There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Value Standards -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Language Assistance Services -[SUMMARY]-> [Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number].] [Tagalog (Tagalog): Kung kailangan ninyo ang tulong sa Tagalog tumawag sa [insert telephone number].] [Chinese (中文): 如果需要中文的帮助，请拨打这个号码 [insert telephone number].] [Navajo (Dine): Dinek'ehgo shika at'ohwol ninisingo, kwijjigo holne' [insert telephone number].] —To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section.—
Coverage Example Scenarios -[ABOUT_COVERAGE_EXAMPLES]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage.

HealthPlan -[OFFERS_WELLNESS_PROGRAM_SAVINGS]-> Wellness Program Cost Reduction Notice
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> Your Grievance and Appeals Rights: There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Value Standards -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Language Assistance Services -[SUMMARY]-> [Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number].] [Tagalog (Tagalog): Kung kailangan ninyo ang tulong sa Tagalog tumawag sa [insert telephone number].] [Chinese (中文): 如果需要中文的帮助，请拨打这个号码 [insert telephone number].] [Navajo (Dine): Dinek'ehgo shika at'ohwol ninisingo, kwijjigo holne' [insert telephone number].] —To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section.—

Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage.

HealthPlan -[OFFERS_WELLNESS_PROGRAM_SAVINGS]-> Wellness Program Cost Reduction Notice
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> Your Grievance and Appeals Rights: There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Value Standards -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.

HealthPlan -[OFFERS_WELLNESS_PROGRAM_SAVINGS]-> Wellness Program Cost Reduction Notice
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> Your Grievance and Appeals Rights: There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Value Standards -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.

HealthPlan -[OFFERS_WELLNESS_PROGRAM_SAVINGS]-> Wellness Program Cost Reduction Notice
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> Your Grievance and Appeals Rights: There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Value Standards -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Language Assistance Services -[SUMMARY]-> [Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number].] [Tagalog (Tagalog): Kung kailangan ninyo ang tulong sa Tagalog tumawag sa [insert telephone number].] [Chinese (中文): 如果需要中文的帮助，请拨打这个号码 [insert telephone number].] [Navajo (Dine): Dinek'ehgo shika at'ohwol ninisingo, kwijjigo holne' [insert telephone number].] —To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section.—
Coverage Example Scenarios -[ABOUT_COVERAGE_EXAMPLES]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage.
Pregnancy & Childbirth Scenario -[CATEGORY]-> MATERNITY

Pregnancy & Childbirth Scenario -[DESCRIPTION]-> (9 months of in-network pre-natal care and a hospital delivery)

Health Coverage Cost Examples -[SUMMARY]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage. HealthPlan -[OFFERS_WELLNESS_PROGRAM_SAVINGS]-> Wellness Program Cost Reduction Notice
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Value Standards -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Language Assistance Services -[SUMMARY]-> [Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number].] [Tagalog (Tagalog): Kung kailangan ninyo ang tulong sa Tagalog tumawag sa [insert telephone number].] [Chinese (中文): 如果需要中文的帮助，请拨打这个号码 [insert telephone number].] [Navajo (Dine): Dinek'ehgo shika at'ohwol ninisingo, kwijjigo holne' [insert telephone number].] —To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section.—

Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage.

HealthPlan -[OFFERS_WELLNESS_PROGRAM_SAVINGS]-> Wellness Program Cost Reduction Notice
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Rights to Continue Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you want to continue your coverage after it ends. The contact information for those agencies is: [insert State, HHS, DOL, and/or other applicable agency contact information]. Other options to continue coverage are available to you too, including buying individual insurance coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace. For more information about the Marketplace, visit www.HealthCare.gov or call 1-800-318-2596.
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Value Standards -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Language Assistance Services -[SUMMARY]-> [Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number].] [Tagalog (Tagalog): Kung kailangan ninyo ang tulong sa Tagalog tumawag sa [insert telephone number].] [Chinese (中文): 如果需要中文的帮助，请拨打这个号码 [insert telephone number].] [Navajo (Dine): Dinek'ehgo shika at'ohwol ninisingo, kwijjigo holne' [insert telephone number].] —To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section.—

Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage.

HealthPlan -[OFFERS_WELLNESS_PROGRAM_SAVINGS]-> Wellness Program Cost Reduction Notice
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?

Rights to Continue Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you want to continue your coverage after it ends. The contact information for those agencies is: [insert State, HHS, DOL, and/or other applicable agency contact information]. Other options to continue coverage are available to you too, including buying individual insurance coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace. For more information about the Marketplace, visit www.HealthCare.gov or call 1-800-318-2596.
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Value Standards -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.

HealthPlan -[COVERAGE_FOR]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
What is the overall deductible? -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
What is the overall deductible? -[ANSWER]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
What is the overall deductible? -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
What is the overall deductible? -[CHUNK_ID]-> iq_000
Are there services covered before you meet your deductible? -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Are there services covered before you meet your deductible? -[ANSWER]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Are there services covered before you meet your deductible? -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Are there services covered before you meet your deductible? -[CHUNK_ID]-> iq_001
Are there other deductibles for specific services? -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Are there other deductibles for specific services? -[ANSWER]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Are there other deductibles for specific services? -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Are there other deductibles for specific services? -[CHUNK_ID]-> iq_002
What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan? -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan? -[ANSWER]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan? -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan? -[CHUNK_ID]-> iq_003
What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit? -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit? -[ANSWER]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit? -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit? -[CHUNK_ID]-> iq_004
Will you pay less if you use a network provider? -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Will you pay less if you use a network provider? -[ANSWER]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Will you pay less if you use a network provider? -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Will you pay less if you use a network provider? -[CHUNK_ID]-> iq_005
Do you need a referral to see a specialist? -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Do you need a referral to see a specialist? -[ANSWER]-> Yes.
Do you need a referral to see a specialist? -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Do you need a referral to see a specialist? -[CHUNK_ID]-> iq_006
Primary care visit to treat an injury or illness | network_cost -[VALUE]-> $35 copay/office visit and 20% coinsurance for other outpatient services; deductible does not apply
Primary care visit to treat an injury or illness | out_of_network_cost -[VALUE]-> 40% coinsurance
Specialist visit | network_cost -[VALUE]-> $50 copay/visit
Specialist visit | out_of_network_cost -[VALUE]-> 40% coinsurance
Specialist visit | Limitation -[TEXT]-> Preauthorization is required. Failure to obtain preauthorization may result in a reduction in benefits by 50% of the total cost of the service.

Your Rights to Continue Coverage: -[CONTENT]-> There are agencies that can help if you want to continue your coverage after it ends. The contact information for those agencies is: [insert State, HHS, DOL, and/or other applicable agency contact information]. Other options to continue coverage are available to you too, including buying individual insurance coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace. For more information about the Marketplace, visit www.HealthCare.gov or call 1-800-318-2596.
Your Grievance and Appeals Rights: -[CONTENT]-> There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Does this plan provide Minimum Essential Coverage? -[CONTENT]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Does this plan meet Minimum Value Standards? -[CONTENT]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.

Deductible -[TYPE]-> ImportantQuestion
Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Deductible -[AMOUNT_INDIVIDUAL]-> $500
Deductible -[AMOUNT_FAMILY]-> $1,000
Deductible -[DESCRIPTION]-> Amount that must be paid before the plan begins to pay
Pre-Deductible Coverage -[TYPE]-> ImportantQuestion
Pre-Deductible Coverage -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Pre-Deductible Coverage -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Pre-Deductible Coverage -[COVERED_SERVICES]-> Preventive care and primary care services
Pre-Deductible Coverage -[COST_SHARING]-> Copayment or coinsurance may apply
Pre-Deductible Coverage -[DEDUCTIBLE_EXCEPTION]-> Yes

Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[NETWORK_INDIVIDUAL_LIMIT]-> $2,500

Out-of-Pocket Limit Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.

Network Provider -[TYPE]-> ImportantQuestion
Network Provider -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Network Provider -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider -[USES_NETWORK]-> true
Network Provider -[NETWORK_PROVIDER_LIST]-> www.[insert].com or 1-800-[insert]
Network Provider -[OUT_OF_NETWORK_BILLING]-> balance bill possible

Minimum Essential Coverage -[TYPE]-> Section
Minimum Essential Coverage -[TITLE]-> Does this plan provide Minimum Essential Coverage?
Minimum Essential Coverage -[CONTENT]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Essential Coverage -[COVERAGE_STATUS]-> yes
Minimum Essential Coverage -[EXEMPTION_INFO]-> available for qualified individuals
Minimum Essential Coverage -[TAX_IMPLICATION]-> payment required for non-coverage months

Minimum Value Standards -[CONTENT]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Minimum Value Standards -[MEETS_MINIMUM_VALUE]-> Yes

Deductible Amount -[TYPE]-> ImportantQuestion
Deductible Amount -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Deductible Amount -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Deductible Amount -[AMOUNT]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Deductible Amount -[TYPE]-> overall
Deductible Amount -[APPLIES_TO]-> individual and family
Deductible Exemptions -[TYPE]-> ImportantQuestion
Deductible Exemptions -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Deductible Exemptions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Deductible Exemptions -[EXEMPT_SERVICES]-> Preventive care and primary care services
Deductible Exemptions -[COST_SHARING]-> Copayment or coinsurance may apply
Deductible Exemptions -[EXEMPTION_DETAILS]-> Certain preventive services are covered without cost-sharing and before deductible is met
Specific Service Deductibles -[TYPE]-> ImportantQuestion
Specific Service Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Specific Service Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Specific Service Deductibles -[DEDUCTIBLE_TYPE]-> prescription drug coverage, occupational therapy services
Specific Service Deductibles -[DEDUCTIBLE_AMOUNT]-> $300
Specific Service Deductibles -[DESCRIPTION]-> pay all costs up to deductible before plan pays
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[TYPE]-> ImportantQuestion
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[INDIVIDUAL_NETWORK_LIMIT]-> $2,500
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[FAMILY_NETWORK_LIMIT]-> $5,000
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[INDIVIDUAL_OUT_OF_NETWORK_LIMIT]-> $4,000
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[FAMILY_OUT_OF_NETWORK_LIMIT]-> $8,000
Out-of-Pocket Limit Exclusions -[TYPE]-> ImportantQuestion
Out-of-Pocket Limit Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Out-of-Pocket Limit Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Limit Exclusions -[EXCLUDED_EXPENSES]-> Copayments, premiums, balance-billed charges, non-covered services
Out-of-Pocket Limit Exclusions -[IMPACT_ON_LIMIT]-> Do not count toward out-of-pocket limit
Network Provider Savings -[TYPE]-> ImportantQuestion
Network Provider Savings -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Network Provider Savings -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Savings -[NETWORK_PROVIDER_SAVINGS]-> Yes
Network Provider Savings -[NETWORK_PROVIDER_LIST_SOURCE]-> website and phone number
Network Provider Savings -[BALANCE_BILLING_RISK]-> Yes
Network Provider Savings -[OUT_OF_NETWORK_PROVIDER_IMPACT]-> Higher costs

Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.

Minimum Essential Coverage -[TYPE]-> Section
Minimum Essential Coverage -[TITLE]-> Does this plan provide Minimum Essential Coverage?
Minimum Essential Coverage -[CONTENT]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Essential Coverage -[COVERAGE_STATUS]-> Yes
Minimum Essential Coverage -[TAX_IMPLICATION]-> Payment required when filing tax return if not covered

Deductible Amount -[TYPE]-> ImportantQuestion
Deductible Amount -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Deductible Amount -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Deductible Amount -[AMOUNT_INDIVIDUAL]-> $500
Deductible Amount -[AMOUNT_FAMILY]-> $1,000
Deductible Amount -[DESCRIPTION]-> Amount that must be paid before the plan begins to pay
Pre-Deductible Coverage -[TYPE]-> ImportantQuestion
Pre-Deductible Coverage -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Pre-Deductible Coverage -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Pre-Deductible Coverage -[COVERED_SERVICES]-> Preventive care and primary care services
Pre-Deductible Coverage -[COST_SHARING]-> Copayment or coinsurance may apply
Pre-Deductible Coverage -[ADDITIONAL_INFO]-> https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/

Out-of-Pocket Limit -[TYPE]-> ImportantQuestion
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[NETWORK_INDIVIDUAL]-> $2,500
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[NETWORK_FAMILY]-> $5,000
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[OUT_OF_NETWORK_INDIVIDUAL]-> $4,000

Out-of-Pocket Limit Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Out-of-Pocket Limit Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Limit Exclusions -[EXCLUDED_EXPENSES]-> Copayments, premiums, balance-billed charges, non-covered services
Out-of-Pocket Limit Exclusions -[IMPACT_ON_LIMIT]-> Do not count toward out-of-pocket limit
Network Provider -[TYPE]-> ImportantQuestion
Network Provider -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Network Provider -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider -[USES_NETWORK]-> true
Network Provider -[NETWORK_PROVIDER_LIST]-> www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert]
Network Provider -[OUT_OF_NETWORK_IMPACT]-> balance bill possible

Minimum Essential Coverage -[TYPE]-> Section
Minimum Essential Coverage -[TITLE]-> Does this plan provide Minimum Essential Coverage?
Minimum Essential Coverage -[CONTENT]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Essential Coverage -[COVERAGE_STATUS]-> Yes
Minimum Essential Coverage -[TAX_IMPLICATION]-> Payment required when filing tax return if not covered
Minimum Essential Coverage -[EXEMPTION_AVAILABLE]-> Yes

Minimum Value Standards -[CONTENT]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
